title: Police searching for tandem moped riders after shots fired in Roxbury
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston police seek two moped riders in connection with June 30 shots-fired incident in Roxbury.
The suspects were photographed wearing black masks on Schuyler Street heading toward Elm Hill Avenue.
Police urge anyone with information to contact Boston detectives at (617) 343-4275.

title: Jaylen Brown comforts 6-year-old fan whose reaction to trade went viral
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown met with 6-year-old Giovanni Jean at his M.I.T. summer program after a viral reaction to Brown's trade.
Jean, distressed by Brown's trade to the 76ers, expressed admiration and offered a handwritten message of support.
Brown, a former Celtic, reassured young Jean with a hug and friendship promise despite moving to Philadelphia.

title: Fatal Cambridge shooting renews criticism of council vote to end ShotSpotter contract
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Xavier Bautista, 32, was shot dead in Cambridge around 4:30 a.m. on July 4.
The shooting renewed criticism of the Cambridge City Council's May vote to end ShotSpotter use.
Cambridge officials and police unions suggest ShotSpotter could have hastened emergency response to Bautista.

title: Jaylen Brown on Celtics trade: Boston is home and no organization can take that away
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown traded from Boston Celtics to Philadelphia 76ers, maintains strong connection to Boston.
Brown's 7uice Foundation Day declared in Massachusetts, emphasizing his community impact beyond basketball.
Brown plans to continue community work in Boston and Atlanta despite playing for a different team.

title: Wrong-way I-95 driver faces drunk driving charges after crash, police say
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: John Tarshi, 25, of Lawrence, arrested for drunk driving after a wrong-way crash on I-95 in Woburn.
Police discovered a forged RMV document in the Porsche he crashed, further charges are being considered.
Tarshi refused field sobriety and breathalyzer tests; no injuries reported from the incident.

title: All the new movies and TV shows streaming now (July 2026)
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Jennifer Garner stars in 'The Five-Star Weekend', debuting on Peacock July 9, filmed on Nantucket.
HBO Max marks Shark Week with additional shark content starting July 26, celebrating its made-up holiday.
Boston-filmed 'The Drama' premieres on HBO Max on July 31; 'Project Hail Mary' streams on Prime Video July 3.

title: Noah Kahan’s historic 4-show Fenway Park run kicks off Tuesday. Here’s everything you need to know.
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: Noah Kahan begins his historic four-show run at Fenway Park, with performances scheduled for Tuesday, Wednesday, Friday, and Saturday.
Fenway Park concerts will feature fireworks each night, weather permitting, confirmed by the Boston Fire Department.
Boston businesses offer Noah Kahan-themed items and events, benefiting his mental health initiative, The Busyhead Project.

title: Labouré College cuts 65 jobs ahead of Curry College transition
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Labouré College will lay off 65 employees as it prepares to close its Milton campus and transfer programs.
Curry College will absorb Labouré’s nursing programs and launch a new center for healthcare opportunities.
Curry plans to hire 35 current Labouré staff and faculty as part of the transition.

title: Celtics sign Ron Harper Jr. to multi-year contract
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics sign Ron Harper Jr. to a multi-year contract worth up to $13.7 million over four years.
Harper Jr. played 29 games for the Celtics this season, averaging 4.2 points and 1.7 rebounds.
He averaged 25.4 points with the Maine Celtics and earned third-team All-NBA G-League honors.

title: ‘Amateur hour’: Mass. sheriff accuses ICE of trying to bully him into breaking the law
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Bristol County Sheriff Paul Heroux accuses ICE of pressuring him to violate state and federal laws by honoring detainers.
ICE criticized Bristol County for releasing a Honduran man on bail, blaming 'sanctuary politicians' for endangering public safety.
Heroux insists Massachusetts law supports non-compliance with ICE detainers, highlighting the need for convictions before deportation.
